data.title: Beam Global targets positive cash flow in 2025 with expanded product lineup and geographic reach
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Beam Global (BEEM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Desmond Wheatley noted that Beam Global’s 2024 revenue doubled compared to previous years, reaching $49.3 million, and emphasized the company's five-year revenue CAGR of 68%. He attributed this to investments in sales resources and engineering improvements that reduced costs.
  • The company achieved gross margins of 15%, with adjusted gross margins net of non-cash items at 21%. Wheatley highlighted that cost-saving measures, including material discounts and operational efficiencies, contributed to this improvement.
  • Wheatley announced the launch of new products like BeamSpot, BeamBike, BeamWell, and BeamPatrol, designed to diversify revenue streams. He emphasized that these new offerings cater to distinct market needs such as disaster zones, urban mobility, and law enforcement.
  • CFO Lisa Potok stated that cash on hand was $4.6 million at the end of 2024, compared to $10.4 million in 2023, with cash use focused on acquisitions and operational needs.
  • Wheatley discussed the strategic importance of Beam’s geographic expansion, particularly in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, as a response to challenges in the U.S. market.

Outlook

  • Management projected that the company is on a trajectory to achieve positive cash flow in 2025, even with flat revenue compared to 2024. Wheatley indicated that gross profit trends and operational efficiencies will support this goal.
  • Wheatley mentioned that the first quarter of 2025 is expected to reflect similar challenges as Q4 2024, but the company anticipates growth in later quarters driven by its expanded product lineup and international sales focus.

Financial Results

  • Beam Global reported a loss from operations of $11.7 million for 2024, down from $16.3 million in 2023. Adjusted non-GAAP loss from operations was $8.9 million in 2024 compared to $11.8 million in 2023.
  • Gross profit for 2024 was $10.4 million, with significant contributions from cost reductions and operational efficiencies.
  • Operating expenses increased to $19 million in 2024, primarily due to new hires, marketing expenses, and acquisition-related costs.
  • Recurring revenue initiatives, such as sponsorship models, have begun contributing to the company’s income, with initial success at Belgrade International Airport.

Q&A

  • Tate Sullivan, Maxim Group: Asked about new product timelines and sales expectations. Wheatley responded that products like BeamBike and BeamWell have already seen initial sales, with BeamWell units en route to the Middle East.
  • Sullivan also inquired about recurring revenue sources. Wheatley stated that sponsorship models and data services from smart city products are key areas for future recurring revenue growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the potential of new products and international expansion while acknowledging challenges in the U.S. federal market.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing resilience and strategic diversification to mitigate risks. Wheatley reiterated, “We are on track to achieve positive cash flow this year.”

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue for Q4 2024 declined compared to Q3, reflecting reduced federal sales and order timing issues. However, enterprise sales increased, making up 64% of Q4 orders versus 47.9% in Q3.
  • Gross margin improved year-over-year, but quarterly variability remained due to lower volumes impacting overhead absorption.
  • Management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to a more assertive focus on international and product diversification in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Wheatley identified challenges from U.S. federal policy changes, particularly reduced government incentives for electric vehicles and infrastructure.
  • Management highlighted tariffs and rising material costs as potential risks but noted the mitigating impact of European operations, which allow for cost-efficient production outside the U.S.
  • Analysts raised concerns about delayed UL certifications, which have impacted order fulfillment timelines.

Final Takeaway

Beam Global’s Q4 2024 earnings call showcased the company’s focus on mitigating U.S. market challenges through international expansion and a diversified product portfolio. With innovative offerings like BeamBike and BeamWell, alongside strategic cost-saving measures, the company is positioned to achieve positive cash flow in 2025. Management’s emphasis on recurring revenue and geographic reach underscores its resilience amid federal policy uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gold’s latest record-breaking run spurred by buying rush in daily auctions
data.description:
This week's surge in gold prices to fresh records was fueled by a rush of buying activity in daily auctions, a sign that major players in the market are still adding to their holdings even at all-time high prices, Bloomberg reported Friday.

Bids for more than 400K oz of gold worth $1.3B were registered in the opening round of the afternoon auction on Thursday, the largest such volume since September 2019, according to a Bloomberg analysis of data from ICE Benchmark Administration, which runs the auctions.

Traders and analysts usually relate major buying or selling activity in the auctions to central banks, which have been a major driver in gold prices rising above $3,000/oz, but other groups of market participants also have been big buyers recently, with ETFs adding 5.8M oz of gold YTD, Bloomberg said.

Gold futures settled above $3,200/oz for the first time on Friday, as a weakening dollar and escalating U.S.-China trade war ramped up recession fears, sending investors flocking to the traditional safety of the metal.

"Gold is clearly seen as the favored safe-haven asset in a world upended by Trump's trade war," WisdomTree commodities strategist Nitesh Shah said. "The U.S. dollar has depreciated, and U.S. Treasuries are selling off hard, as faith in the U.S. as a reliable trading partner has diminished."

Elsewhere, a softer than expected Consumer Price Index report has raised hopes the Federal Reserve will ease monetary policy, which would be another boon for bullion.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed +7% this week at a new record-high settlement of $3,222.20/oz, a fourth consecutive daily gain and the largest one-week percentage gain since the week ending March 27, 2020, while front-month April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +9.3% to $31.824/oz for the week, its best weekly percentage gain since the week ending September 13, 2024; on Friday, gold and silver rose 2.1% and 3.7%, respectively.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Current gold prices look well-supported given market turmoil caused by U.S. tariff policy, Commerzbank analysts said in raising their year-end price forecast for spot gold to $3,000/oz from $2,850/oz previously, and UBS analysts now expect gold prices will hit $3,500/oz this year, expecting gold's rally will "extend into next year and for prices to stabilize at higher levels further out."

Earlier this week, Deutsche Bank raised its average gold price forecasts for 2025 and 2026 to $3,139/oz and $3,700/oz, respectively.

Gold ETFs posted strong inflows of 92 metric tons in March, bringing Q1 inflows to a three-year high of 226 tons, with the biggest inflows coming from U.S.-listed ETFs, although European and Asian ETFs also showed inflows.

data.title: Trump administration looking at steep budget cuts at NOAA
data.description:
The White House is considering major cuts to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration as it seeks to end most of the agency’s work on climate change, Axios reported Friday, citing an internal document.

According to the report, the document indicates the Trump administration is looking at $1.7B in cuts to NOAA’s overall budget of $4.5B.

The document reportedly suggests that the agency’s Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research be eliminated as a line office and most of its budget, down to around $171M.   

Axios quoted the document as saying “all funding for climate, weather, and ocean Laboratories and Cooperative Institutes” would be included. 

Spire Global (NYSE:SPIR), which provides space-based data, analytics and other services, has a number of contracts with NOAA. The company’s shares closed down nearly 6% on Friday. 

NOAA did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Melcor REIT receives unitholder approval for plan of arrangement
data.description:
• Melcor Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX:MR.UN:CA) said on Friday that it has received the approval from its unitholders to acquire its unowned equity interest, which is about 45%, in Melcor REIT.
  • Melcor will pay $5.50 per Class A LP unit in cash for the acquisition. Its unowned equity interest in the REIT comprises all of the latter's outstanding Class A LP units which is around 13M.
  • The REIT will use the proceeds from the REIT LP Sale to repurchase and cancel all of the its outstanding units.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BNY Mellon outlines growth strategies amid platform transformation and strong Q1 2025 results
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Bank of New York Mellon (BK) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robin Vince highlighted a "solid financial performance" with earnings per share reaching $1.58, representing a 26% year-over-year increase. Total revenue reached $4.8 billion, up 6% year-over-year, driven by fee revenue growth and controlled expenses.
  • Vince emphasized the continued transformation towards a more platforms-oriented company, with over half of BNY Mellon's employees now working under the new operating model. This approach has already improved operational agility and client onboarding times.
  • Carolyn Weinberg has been appointed Chief Product and Innovation Officer to accelerate innovation across areas such as digital assets, instant payments, and private markets. Vince also underscored the company's growing AI capabilities, revealing over 40 AI solutions in production with expectations for productivity gains and risk management improvements.
  • CFO Dermot McDonogh reported a pretax margin of 32% and a return on tangible common equity of 24% for the quarter. He attributed the results to "balanced growth," noting an 11% year-over-year increase in net interest income and controlled cost growth of 2%.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated expectations for mid-single-digit percentage growth in net interest income for full-year 2025. Fee revenue growth was also projected, subject to market dynamics.
  • Expense growth for 2025 is expected to remain within 1% to 2%, excluding notable items, demonstrating ongoing cost discipline. The effective tax rate is forecasted to average 22% to 23% for the full year.
  • The company reaffirmed its intention to return approximately 100% of 2025 earnings to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue of $4.8 billion grew 6% year-over-year, supported by a 3% increase in fee revenues and an 11% rise in net interest income.
  • Assets under custody and/or administration (AUCA) reached $53.1 trillion, reflecting a 9% year-over-year growth driven by client inflows and market gains. Assets under management (AUM) were stable at $2 trillion, despite net outflows of $18 billion in the quarter.
  • Segment highlights included an 11% revenue increase in Market and Wealth Services, supported by strong performance in Pershing and Treasury Services. Securities Services' revenue rose 8% year-over-year, driven by growth in asset servicing and foreign exchange revenue.
  • Provision for credit losses was $18 million, primarily tied to commercial real estate exposure.

Q&A

  • Ken Usdin, Autonomous Research: Asked about deposit trends and liquidity. CFO McDonogh noted a slight sequential decline in noninterest-bearing deposits and stability in overall deposit balances. He emphasized the firm’s conservative balance sheet management.
  • Alex Blostein, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about M&A appetite. CEO Vince indicated openness to strategic acquisitions aligned with the company’s priorities and the potential to integrate efficiently under the platforms model.
  • Brian Bedell, Deutsche Bank: Sought clarification on asset servicing inflows. Management highlighted competitive strength and investments made in servicing capabilities, particularly in ETFs and alternatives.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism but raised questions about macroeconomic uncertainty and potential impacts on deposit trends and fee dynamics.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strategic investments, client wins, and operational improvements. Vince consistently underscored the scalability and resilience provided by the platforms transformation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management appeared more focused on navigating potential market volatility than on immediate growth targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management’s tone on guidance remained consistent, though this quarter reflected increased emphasis on navigating macroeconomic uncertainties.
  • Fee revenue growth continued, but management noted an evolving client activity mix, with stronger volumes in some areas like collateral management.
  • Analysts' questions this quarter delved deeper into operational resilience and the potential for new M&A opportunities, reflecting a shift in focus from purely financial metrics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged uncertainties related to global trade policies, geopolitical risks, and market volatility as key concerns.
  • Analysts noted potential risks tied to deposit mix shifts and elevated credit exposures in commercial real estate.
  • To mitigate these risks, BNY Mellon emphasized its robust balance sheet, liquidity, and focus on operational efficiency.

Final Takeaway

BNY Mellon delivered a solid financial quarter, demonstrating progress in its platforms transformation strategy, which aims to enhance client service and operational efficiency. Strategic hires and AI investments underline its focus on innovation, while conservative financial guidance reflects macroeconomic caution. Despite external uncertainties, management’s confidence in achieving long-term growth and operational leverage remains evident.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Petro-Victory Energy announces short term promissory note
data.description:
• Petro-Victory Energy (TSXV:VRY:CA) said on Friday that it has borrowed US$250,000 and issued an unsecured promissory note to an arm's length third party. 
  • The loan bears interest at an annual rate of 14% per annum until maturity and 18% per annum thereafter.
  • The lender will be issued 285,360 bonus warrants in connection with the loan, with each warrant being exercisable at CAD$1.25 per common share on or before March 26, 2026.

data.title: FTC may reconsider Exxon, Chevron board restrictions
data.description:
The U.S. Federal Trade Commission on Friday took a step toward a potential reversal of bans on certain executives joining the boards of Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) and Chevron (NYSE:CVX) that the Biden administration had made as a condition to letting the companies acquire other oil producers.

Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) had agreed to bar former Pioneer Natural Resources CEO Scott Sheffield from its board when it acquired Pioneer in a $59.5B deal that closed last May, and Chevron (NYSE:CVX) consented to a similar order keeping Hess (HES) CEO John Hess off its board when it bought Hess for $53B in a transaction that has not been finalized.

Both deals were approved by the FTC, then led by Chair Lina Khan, on the condition that Sheffield and Hess be barred from the respective boards.

The FTC said Friday it is seeking public comment on petitions filed by Sheffield, Chevron (CVX) and Hess seeking to reverse the bans, which were implemented based on allegations that the executives engaged in improper communications with OPEC; after the 30-day comment period closes, the agency will vote to determine how to resolve both petitions.

The requests come just weeks after President Trump fired the FTC's two Democratic commissioners and Republican dissenters from the Sheffield and Hess rulings assumed the majority.

data.title: CFPB dismisses lawsuit against Comerica
data.description:
The Consumer Finance Protection Bureau on Friday dropped its lawsuit against Comerica Bank (NYSE:CMA) that had accused the lender of poorly treating enrollees in a Treasury Department program that provided government benefits on prepaid debit cards. 

According to a court filing in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Texas, the regulator said it was dismissing the case without prejudice.

The case was one of the last enforcement actions authorized by former CFPB Director Rohit Chopra during the closing weeks of the Biden administration. 

In its lawsuit, the CFPB accused the Dallas-based bank of disconnecting 24 million service calls deliberately, assessing fees that were illegal, and not timely investigating error claims by cardholders, among other alleged issues. 

Comerica has been contracted with the department since 2008 to deliver prepaid debit cards to those receiving benefits from the federal government under the Direct Express program for recipients without bank accounts. 

Comerica declined comment when reached by Seeking Alpha. A CFPB spokesperson told Seeking Alpha that the agency “is doing its due diligence to work with Comerica.”

data.title: Bristol Myers gets FDA approval for Opdivo, Yervoy combination in liver cancer
data.description:
• Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) said on Friday that the U.S. FDA approved Opdivo plus Yervoy as a treatment for metastatic hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC), a form of liver cancer.
  • Opdivo plus Yervoy was previously granted accelerated approval by FDA in 2020 based on results from early stage trial and has been established as a second-line treatment for advanced HCC, the company said.
  • The  approval is based on late-stage trial where Opdivo plus Yervoy demonstrated a statistically significant overall survival compared to lenvatinib or sorafenib.

data.title: Patterson Companies gains on Nasdaq delisting for sale to Patient Square
data.description:
Patterson Companies (NASDAQ:PDCO) edged higher by 1.1% in after-hours trading after a Nasdaq delisting notice for its sale to Patient Square Capital.

The deal is tentatively scheduled to close before the market open on Thursday and the stock will be halted after the close on Wednesday, according to the Nasdaq delisting notice on Friday.

The Nasdaq notice comes after Patterson (NASDAQ:PDCO) ticked lower by 0.9% on Thursday amid a Bloomberg report that debt financing for the Patient Square deal was delayed with the recent market tumult. A $2.35 billion debt package for the transaction was delayed. 

Patient Square Capital agreed to acquire Patterson (PDCO) for $31.35 a share in cash in December.
